platformDescription: A project management app for modern teams to organize projects, track tasks on Kanban boards, and collaborate.

flows.name: Explore the Dashboard
flows.description: Get oriented with the Dashboard — your home base showing all projects, progress bars, and recent team activity.

steps.title: Review your project cards
steps.instruction: Look at the project cards in the main area. You'll see three projects: 'Website Redesign' (green dot, 65% progress), 'Mobile App v2' (blue dot, 30%), and 'API Integration' (purple dot, 90%). Each card shows a description, task count, and member count.
steps.explanation: Project cards give you a quick overview of all active projects and how far along each one is.

steps.title: Check recent activity
steps.instruction: Scroll down to the 'Recent Activity' section below the project cards. You'll see a feed showing things like Alice completing a task, Bob creating a task, and Carol commenting.
steps.explanation: The activity feed keeps you in the loop on what your team is doing without opening each project.

steps.title: Open a project
steps.instruction: Click the 'Website Redesign' project card to open its Kanban task board.
steps.explanation: Clicking a project card takes you to its task board where you can manage tasks across columns.

flows.name: Navigate the Kanban Board
flows.description: Learn how the task board works — tasks organized in TO DO, IN PROGRESS, and DONE columns.

steps.title: Understand the board columns
steps.instruction: You're now on the Website Redesign project page. Notice the three columns: 'TO DO' on the left, 'IN PROGRESS' in the middle, and 'DONE' on the right. Each task card shows a title, priority badge (High/Medium/Low), and an assignee avatar.
steps.explanation: The Kanban board lets you see the status of every task at a glance.

steps.title: Check task priorities
steps.instruction: Look at the task cards — each has a colored priority label. Red means 'High', orange means 'Medium', and gray means 'Low'. The letter circles on each card show who's assigned.
steps.explanation: Priority labels help the team focus on the most urgent work first.

flows.name: Add a Task
flows.description: Create your first task on the Kanban board using the Add Task button.

steps.title: Click Add Task
steps.instruction: Click the '+ Add Task' button in the top-right corner of the project page. A modal dialog will pop up.
steps.explanation: The Add Task modal lets you quickly create tasks without leaving the board.

steps.title: Fill in the task details
steps.instruction: In the modal: type a task name in the 'Task Title' field (e.g., 'Design new logo'). Pick a priority from the dropdown (Low, Medium, or High). Choose an assignee from the 'Assign to' dropdown.
steps.explanation: Clear titles, priorities, and assignees ensure tasks get done by the right person at the right time.

steps.title: Create the task
steps.instruction: Click the 'Create Task' button at the bottom of the modal. The task will appear in the TO DO column on the board.
steps.explanation: New tasks land in TO DO and can be moved to IN PROGRESS and DONE as work progresses.

flows.name: Create a New Project
flows.description: Start a brand new project from scratch using the New Project form.

steps.title: Go to New Project
steps.instruction: Click '+ New Project' in the left sidebar, or use the '+ New Project' button at the top of the Dashboard.
steps.explanation: This opens the project creation form.

steps.title: Fill in project details
steps.instruction: Type a project name (e.g., 'Marketing Campaign'). Add a description. Choose a visibility level from the dropdown (Team, Private, or Public). Optionally invite members by email.
steps.explanation: A clear name and description help your team find and understand the project.

steps.title: Create the project
steps.instruction: Click the 'Create Project' button. You'll be taken to your new project's empty Kanban board, ready for tasks.
steps.explanation: Your project is now live and visible to your team.

flows.name: Manage Your Team
flows.description: Invite team members and manage roles from the Team page.

steps.title: Open the Team page
steps.instruction: Click 'Team' in the left sidebar. You'll see a list of current team members with their roles and status.
steps.explanation: The Team page is where you manage who has access to your workspace.

steps.title: Invite a new member
steps.instruction: Click the '+ Invite Member' button in the top-right. In the modal, enter an email address, pick a role (Member, Admin, or Viewer), and click 'Send Invite'.
steps.explanation: Invited members receive an email and appear in the list with a 'Pending' badge until they accept.
